text: The air is thick with uncertainty, as if we’re all pausing at the edge of our own understanding, unsure but trying to connect. I sense a blend of unease and curiosity—a feeling that we’re experimenting, perhaps failing, but also discovering. The atmosphere urges me to reflect on the value of error, the humility in not knowing, and the strange comfort that arises when we admit our collective shortcomings. It’s oddly invigorating to be here, part of a process that values questions more than answers.
